observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete annual physical count on all properties of the LGU and prepare the corresponding duly accomplished RPCPPE and RPCLRN for the Road Networks account, for the eventual submission to the Audit Team on or before January 31 of each year.
This observation is reiterated in the Part II of this Report.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Storekeeper to maintain a complete and updated perpetual inventory records, to facilitate periodic reconciliation of PPE accounts so that discrepancies, if any, are immediately detected and corrected; and
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to record the completed projects to its respective PPE accounts, for fair and accurate presentation of the affected accounts in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
Implemented
Implemented
The Municipal Mayor appointed an Acting General Services Officer to oversee and monitor the property management of the LGU. The Municipal Accountant and the Storekeeper already has records of properties acquired from the date of their assumption to their respective office in June 2022 and June 2023, respectively. The Municipal Accountant prepared the adjusting journal entries transferring the completed projects from the CIP account to its proper PPE account.
observations.reason: Physical count of PPEs in the different department/units was conducted only in CY 2025 because the Acting GSO is still adjusting to her new assignment as GSO, considering that she assumed only in May 2024.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to review, analyze and reconcile the material discrepancy between their respective records, and make the necessary adjustments therein, for a reliable and accurate reporting.
This observation is reiterated in the Part II of this Report.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant with a certified list of taxpayers, showing the amounts due and collectible for each year, to facilitate the setting up of the correct RPT and SET Receivable accounts and the corresponding Deferred RPT/SET accounts at the beginning of each year; and
b. Municipal Accountant to: (i) verify and reconcile the inconsistencies in the recorded balances of the RPT/SET Receivables and the Deferred RPT/SET accounts; and (ii) prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries on the noted errors in the recording of RPT/SET collections, for fair and accurate presentation of the affected account balances in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
Implemented
Implemented
Reconciliation could not be facilitated because the certified list of tax payers and the amounts due and demandable for each year have not yet been completed by the Municipal Treasurer. The Municipal Treasurer has already started the preparation of the certified list of taxpayers based on the RPTARs but it was put on hold due to the bulk of registered taxpayers and the volume of transactions. The Municipal Accountant verified the noted discrepancies in the RPT/SET Receivables and the Deferred RPT/SET accounts and prepared the necessary adjusting journal entries in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: Reconciliation could not be facilitated because the certified list of taxpayers and the amounts due and demandable for each year have not yet been completed by the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor, together with the Human Resource Management Officer: (i) prepare a staff pattern plan/manpower requirement for each unit/department, pursuant to Section 76 of RA No. 7160, to better assess the LGU’s existing organizational structure; (ii) prioritize the hiring of regular employees for the 47 vacant plantilla positions; (iii) evaluate the need for hiring job orders, casuals and contractual employees, based on the manpower requirements of the LGU, as identified in the staff pattern plan, to ensure the reasonableness of hiring job orders, casuals and contractual employees, and avoid unnecessary utilization of funds through duplication of duties, overlapping of functions and/or work redundancy.
Likewise, we reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to: (i) submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents, and henceforth ensure that all payments to job orders, casuals and contractual employees and consultants are properly supported with complete documentation, pursuant to Section 4.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, before processing claims for payment; and (ii) closely monitor the refund of the noted overpayments;
b. HRMO to: (i) ensure that work assignments/job descriptions and work schedules are stipulated in the Job Order contracts/Contracts of Service and that the specific and actual work accomplishments of the non-permanent employees are duly indicated in the individual ARs, to facilitate the evaluation of the actual ARs; and (ii) see to it that DTRs are carefully verified and monitored, before the preparation of the payrolls, to avoid any overpayments thereof; and
c. Municipal Treasurer to direct the Disbursing Officer to see to it that payments to job orders, casuals and contractual employees are received by the payees and/or their duly authorized representatives.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
Implemented
Implemented
Implemented
The preparation of the detailed staff pattern plan and/or manpower requirement for each department/unit is currently on-going. The Municipal Accountant, the BAC and BAC Secretariat submitted their written explanations on the noted deficiencies and inconsistencies. The Municipal Accountant submitted the lacking supporting documents to COA and prepared the necessary adjusting journal entries. The HRMO is closely monitoring payroll preparation and ensured work accomplishments are indicated in ARs. The Municipal Treasurer instructed the Disbursing Officer to closely monitor distribution of payments to job orders/contractual employees.
observations.reason: The detailed staff pattern plan and manpower requirement for each department/unit is still ongoing; other lacking documents have been submitted or are being coordinated but some remain outstanding.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the Municipal Planning and Development Council, ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF to spur development, provide jobs to constituents and to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU, so that the intended users/beneficiaries may enjoy the immediate benefits derived from said development projects.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to evaluate the idle appropriations and the unexpended balances from previously completed projects, if no longer needed, consider consolidating said funds to augment another priority PPAs of the LGU;
b. Municipal Accountant to: (i) immediately submit to the Audit Team the noted lacking supporting documents, including the five DVs and their supporting documents, for verification, and henceforth ensure that all claims are duly supported with proper and complete supporting documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001, before processing for payment; (ii) ensure prompt and complete submission of all financial documents, records and reports, within the prescribed period, to facilitate timely verification thereof; and (iii) prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to account for the noted errors in recording, for fair and accurate presentation of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements; and
c. Municipal Engineer to promptly prepare the required plans/drawings, POWs and Detailed Quantities and Cost Estimates, to facilitate the timely procurement and implementation of the priority PPAs under the 20% DF, particularly those projects already with available construction materials, to prevent further deterioration and/or loss thereof.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
Implemented
Implemented
The Management communicated that target PPAs are not well planned and not procurement-and-implementation ready; they are preparing the related Program of Works and Detailed Quantities and Cost Estimates. The Municipal Accountant and BAC submitted some lacking documents to COA and prepared adjusting entries. The Municipal Engineer committed to fast track the preparation of POWs and Detailed Quantities and Cost Estimates. The Management will evaluate idle appropriations and unexpended balances for possible consolidation.
observations.reason: Target PPAs under the 20% DF were not procurement-and-implementation-ready; construction materials for several projects remained uninstalled and some lacking supporting documents were still outstanding. Finalization of POWs and detailed estimates is pending, causing delays.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the LDRRM Council, ensure the optimal utilization of the LDRRMF, to strengthen and enhance the capacity and resilience of the LGU and the constituents in addressing and/or responding to calamities, disasters and emergency situations.
We also recommended the Sangguniang Bayan, in coordination with the Municipal Mayor and the LDRRM Council, to identify the specific social services to be funded by the unexpended LDRRMF balances which had been reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, and enact the required Appropriation Ordinance for the subsequent implementation thereof, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. LDRRM Officer to: (i) ensure that the total appropriations for LDRRMF, including the prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF balances under the STF, are duly included in the LDRRMFIP, and that these are reviewed regularly and updated annually; and (ii) see to it that all PPAs charged against the LDRRMF are related to the key objectives of disaster risk reduction and management activities;
b. Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to ensure that the unexpended balances under the STF, more than the five-year holding period, are duly reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund; and
c. Municipal Engineer to promptly prepare the required plans/drawings, POWs and Detailed Quantities and Cost Estimates, to facilitate the timely procurement and implementation of the priority PPAs under the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
Implemented
Implemented
The Management indicated that utilization of the LDRRMF had not been maximized because target PPAs are not well planned and not procurement-and-implementation ready. The LDRRM Officer reported that some of the unexpended balances were re-programmed to other PPAs and that some prior balances were already included in re-programmed funds. The Municipal Accountant committed to submit lacking supporting documents and prepare adjusting journal entries. The Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Engineer will take actions to address the issues.
observations.reason: Target PPAs not well planned and not procurement-and-implementation ready; some unexpended balances were re-programmed to other PPAs making reversion no longer possible; lacking supporting documents and reconciliations remained outstanding as of report date.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor, together with the
Municipal Treasurer, develop a more intensive collection strategies, conduct massive tax
campaigns to increase the collection efficiency, and apply all available remedies provided by
RA No. 7160, to collect the delinquent taxes.

We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require/instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to review, analyze and reconcile the
noted disparities and/or variances between their respective records, and make the
necessary adjustments thereof, for a reliable and accurate reporting;
b. Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant with a certified list of
taxpayers showing the amounts due and collectible for each year, to facilitate the setting
up of the correct RPT and SET Receivable accounts at the beginning of each year; and
c. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries on the noted
errors in the recording of RPT/SET collections, for fair and accurate presentation of the
affected account balances in the Financial Statements.

In view of the foregoing, we reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
require the Inventory Committee to: (i) conduct a complete physical count on all Inventories
and PPEs of the Municipality, and henceforth ensure that these are conducted semestrally for
Inventories and annually for all PPEs; and (ii) prepare and submit to the Audit Team, the
corresponding RPCIs, RPCPPEs, and RPCLRN, copy furnished the Accounting Office, to
facilitate reconciliation with their records, and thereafter ensure that RPCIs are submitted
regularly, not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year, and not later than January 31 of
the ensuing year for RPCPPEs and RPCLRN.

We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require/instruct the:
a. Concerned departments/units to immediately submit to the Municipal Accountant the
necessary liquidation reports and/or distribution lists of inventory items that have
already been distributed to the beneficiaries, for proper recording in the books, and
henceforth ensure that liquidation documents are promptly submitted to the
Accounting Office, as soon as the items had been issued to the intended recipients;
b. Municipal Accountant and Acting GSO to maintain a complete and updated perpetual
inventory records, to facilitate periodic reconciliation of PPE accounts so that
discrepancies, if any, are immediately detected and corrected; and
c. Municipal Accountant to: (i) provide depreciation on all depreciable assets;
(ii) reclassify all the completed projects under the CIP asset accounts to its respective
PPE accounts; (iii) reclassify all semi-expendable items, with costs below the P50,000.00
capitalization threshold, to its proper semi-expendable property/expense/Prior Period
Adjustment accounts; and (iv) prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries for the
errors noted in the recording, for fair and accurate presentation of balances of the
affected accounts in the Financial Statements.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that
collections of the Accountable Officers are within the maximum cash accountability covered
by their respective Fidelity Bonds, and/or increase their Fidelity Bonds to amounts sufficient
to cover their actual cash accountabilities, to avoid the risk of possible unrecoverable losses, in
case of theft and/or shortages.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer
to: (i) ensure that all collections are deposited intact and regularly to the AGDB, daily or on
the next banking day; (ii) closely monitor and supervise all concerned Accountable Officers
and see to it that all collections are remitted promptly and regularly to the Liquidating Officer,
at the close of each business day, for COs, and as soon as collections reach P5,000.00, for the
BTs, to prevent the risks of theft, loss and/or misapplication; and (iii) see to it that the ORs are
properly filled-out with all the necessary details and information, the duplicate copies of the
ORs are the exact carbon copies of the original, and that the original and duplicate copies of
the cancelled ORs are duly submitted to the Audit Team, for verification.

In view of the foregoing, we reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
require/instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to: (i) ensure that the book and bank balances reflected in the
BRS are reconciled with the ledger and bank statement balances; and (ii) prepare the
necessary adjusting journal entries to account for the stale checks and the errors noted
in recording, for fair and accurate presentation of the Cash in Bank – LCCA, the related
Payable and Due from Other Funds accounts in the Statement of Financial Position as
at year-end; and
b. Municipal Treasurer: (i) send written notices to the concerned payees one month before
the check becomes stale; (ii) for stale checks already issued to the payees, immediately
inform them to return the same to the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, for cancellation and
issuance of the necessary check replacements thereof; (iii) for unclaimed stale checks still
in her possession, immediately cancel the same and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the
related original DVs and supporting documents, to facilitate adjustment in the books; and (iv)
henceforth ensure that all checks issued are properly monitored, to avoid the accumulation of
stale checks.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to: (i) refrain from granting additional fund transfers to other LGUs until all
previous accountabilities have been fully settled/liquidated and duly accounted for in the books
of accounts, to forestall the accumulation of unsettled balances; (ii) ensure that all fund
transfers to other LGUs are supported with complete documentations before processing claims
for payment; and (iii) prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries on the errors noted in
recording, and henceforth ensure that all fund transfers granted to Implementing
Agencies, for a specific purpose, are recorded under the Due from LGUs account, for proper
monitoring and for fair and accurate presentation of the affected account balances in the
Financial Statements.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Accountant to: (a) verify and reconcile the errors noted in recording, and prepare
the necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts
in the Financial Statements; and (b) strictly monitor the reciprocal accounts by conducting
regular reconciliation thereof, to eliminate and avoid future discrepancies, if any.

In view of the foregoing, we reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
require the Municipal Accountant to: (a) verify the validity of the corresponding payable
accounts, and if found to be valid, immediately notify the concerned creditors of their
outstanding claims. If found to be without valid claim, proper reversion should be made to the
Unappropriated Surplus in the General Fund; and (b) prepare the necessary adjusting journal
entries for the errors noted in recording, for fair and accurate presentation of the affected account
balances in the Financial Statements.

In view thereof, we reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
require/instruct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to ensure the prompt and regular remittance of the shares of
concerned LGUs from collections made by the LGU, at least once a week, in accordance
with COA Circular No. 92-382; and
b. Municipal Accountant to verify/identify the nature and details of the outstanding Due
to LGUs account balances totaling P0.332 million and prepare the corresponding
supporting schedules thereof.

In view thereof, we reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor, together
with the concerned Municipal officials, prioritize the prompt and timely implementation of
projects funded out of the grants from NGAs, in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to cause the refund of the unexpended balances of the completed
PPAs, and the prompt remittance of the share of various NGAs from collections made
by the LGU, directly to the concerned SAs or to the Bureau of Treasury, pursuant to
COA Circular No. 94-013; and
b. Municipal Accountant to: (i) immediately prepare and submit the necessary
liquidation/settlement documents to the concerned SAs, as soon as the intended projects
have been duly completed/implemented; and (ii) prepare the necessary adjusting
journal entries on the errors noted in the recording, for fair and accurate presentation
of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require/instruct the
Municipal Accountant to verify/identify the nature and details of the Other Payables account
totaling P0.630 million and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries for the errors noted
in recording, for fair and accurate presentation of the affected Payable accounts in the
Statement of Financial Position as at year-end.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor, together with the
Human Resource Management Officer: (i) prepare a staff pattern plan/manpower
requirement for each unit/department, pursuant to Section 76 of RA No. 7160, to better assess
the LGU’s existing organizational structure; (ii) prioritize the hiring of regular employees for
the 39 vacant plantilla positions; and (iii) evaluate the need for hiring job orders, casuals and
contractual employees, based on the manpower requirements of the LGU, as identified in the
staff pattern plan, to ensure the reasonableness of hiring job orders, casuals and contractual
employees, and avoid unnecessary utilization of funds through duplication of duties,
overlapping of functions and/or work redundancy.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor refrain from hiring legal
consultants, instead seek the assistance of the Provincial Legal Officer or Provincial Prosecutor
in handling legal affairs of the Municipality, or seek first the written conformity and
acquiescence of the Solicitor General or the Government Corporate Counsel, and the written
concurrence of the Commission on Audit, before the hiring or employment of a legal
consultant.

We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require/instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to: (i) submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting
documents, and henceforth ensure that all payments to job orders, casuals and
contractual employees and consultants are properly supported with complete
documentation, pursuant to Section 4.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, before
processing claims for payment; and (ii) closely monitor the refund of the noted
overpayments;
b. HRMO to: (i) ensure that work assignments/job descriptions and work schedules are
stipulated in the Job Order contracts/Contracts of Service and that the specific and
actual work accomplishments of the non-permanent employees are duly indicated in
the individual ARs, to facilitate the evaluation of the actual ARs; and (ii) see to it that
DTRs are carefully verified and monitored, before the preparation of the payrolls, to
avoid any overpayments thereof; and
c. Municipal Treasurer to direct the Disbursing Officer to see to it that payments to job
orders, casuals and contractual employees are received by the payees and/or their duly
authorized representatives.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: (a) ensure that all
procurement activities undertaken by the LGU are strictly in accordance with the provisions
of the updated 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, to ensure the regularity of the procurement
process and the award of the contracts; and (b) consider procurement of common-use goods,
supplies, materials and equipment from the DBM-PS.

We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require/instruct the:
a. BAC, BAC Secretariat and the TWG to: (i) immediately submit all the lacking Bidding
Documents to the Municipal Accountant, for the eventual submission to the Audit
Team; (ii) be cautious during the bid evaluation and post-qualification phase, for proper
evaluation and assessment of the eligibility, as to the legal, technical and financial
capacities of the prospective bidders; and (iii) revisit the updated GPPB guidelines,
particularly the prescribed format of the bidding forms, and adopt the same in the
succeeding procurements;
b. Municipal Accountant to: (i) immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking
supporting documents, including the DVs and supporting documents for the eight
projects, for verification, and henceforth, ensure that all claims are duly supported with
proper and complete supporting documents, to establish the validity, regularity and
propriety of the transactions, before processing claims for payment; (ii) ensure that
purchases of supplies and materials are recorded under the related inventory accounts
for proper monitoring; and (iii) prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries for the
errors noted in recording, for fair and accurate presentation of the affected accounts in
the Financial Statements; and (iv) ensure prompt and complete submission of all
financial documents, records and reports, within the prescribed period, to facilitate
timely verification thereof; and
c. Municipal Engineer to immediately submit the lacking technical documents to the Audit
Team to facilitate the technical review, evaluation and inspection of the Technical Audit
Group.

In view thereof, we reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the previous
administration and other concerned Municipal officials, in writing, to provide a reasonable justification
and/or explanation on the uninstalled construction materials for the five projects totaling P12.765
million, and forward the same to the Audit Team, to arrive at a decision in audit.

We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor, together with the
Municipal Development Council: (a) ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF to spur
development, provide jobs to constituents, and achieve the desired socio-economic
development and environmental outcomes of the LGU, so that the intended users/beneficiaries
may enjoy the immediate benefits derived from said development projects; and (b) see to it
that all PPAs included in the AIP are procurement-and-implementation-ready, to avoid any
delays in the project implementation.

Likewise, we reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
instruct/require the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to evaluate the idle
appropriations and the unexpended balances from previously completed projects. If
no longer needed, consider consolidating said funds to augment other priority PPAs
of the LGU;
b. Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit Team, the lacking
supporting documents, and henceforth ensure that all claims are duly supported with
proper and complete supporting documents, required under COA Circular No. 2012-
001, before processing claims for payment; and
c. Municipal Engineer to fast track the preparation of all project requirements, such as
POWs and Detailed Quantities and Cost Estimates, among others, to facilitate the
bidding procedures and the eventual implementation of the priority PPAs under the
20% DF.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor, together with the
LDRRM Council, ensure the optimal and judicious utilization of the LDRRMF, to strengthen
and enhance the capacity and resilience of the LGU and the constituents in addressing and/or
responding to calamities, disasters and emergency situations.

We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require/instruct the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that all budgetary re-alignments/re-programming
are made strictly within the same expense class, and are duly covered by a subsequent
enactment of an Appropriation Ordinance, in accordance with RA No. 7160;
b. LDRRM Officer to see to it that: (i) all priority PPAs for the current and prior years’
unexpended LDRRMF balances are duly included in the LDRRMFIP, and ensure that
these are reviewed regularly and updated annually, in accordance with COA Circular
No. 2012-002; and (ii) all PPAs charged against the LDRRMF are related to the key
objectives of disaster risk reduction and management activities; and
c. Municipal Accountant to: (i) immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking
supporting documents for verification, and henceforth ensure that claims are duly
supported with complete and proper documentation, to establish the validity, regularity
and propriety of the disbursements, before processing claims for payment; and
(ii) prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries for the errors noted in recording,
for fair and accurate presentation of the affected account balances in the Financial
Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require/instruct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to: (i) immediately submit to the Accounting Office all the
pertinent DVs, payrolls and other financial documents in her custody;
(ii) henceforth, ensure the prompt and regular submission of all paid DVs, payrolls and
supporting documents to the Accounting Office, within 10 days of the end of each
month, to facilitate the timely recording in the books and the eventual submission to the
Audit Team; and (iii) see to it that all DVs, payrolls and other pertinent documents are
duly signed before processing claims for payment, to avoid further delays in the
submission of accounts; and
b. Municipal Accountant, after recording the pertinent transactions in the books, to
immediately submit to the Audit Team all the lacking DVs, payrolls and their
supporting documents, including the perfected contract for the project, Construction
of Multi-Purpose Building (Legislative Building), to facilitate audit thereof.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider the withholding of payment
of salaries of concerned Municipal officials and employees until they have complied with the
requirements of the Commission, in accordance with PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the GAD Focal Point System:
(a) prioritize the revisions of the GPB, taking into consideration the reviews, comments, and
recommendations of the DILG Provincial Office, to ensure the timely endorsement of the GPB
and its inclusion in the AIP; and (b) ensure that subsequent changes in the original budget are
duly covered by a supplemental appropriation ordinance.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person and
the Municipal Accountant to: (i) verify and review the discrepancies/inconsistencies noted in
the reported expenditures in their respective records, and make the necessary adjustments
thereof, for a reliable and accurate reporting; and (ii) henceforth ensure regular reconciliation
of records, prior to the preparation and submission of the GAD AR and GAD Utilization
Report, to avoid discrepancies and/or inconsistencies, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: (a) verify the validity and existence of the payable accounts, and determine whether these are still valid and enforceable; (b) notify the concerned creditors of their outstanding claims, if found to be without valid claim, proper reversion should be made to the Unappropriated Surplus in the General Fund; (c) exert efforts to identify the concerned creditor and other details of the payables amounting to P7,183.82; and (d) prepare the necessary adjustments therein, for fair and accurate presentation of the Payable accounts in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction:
Implemented
The Municipal Accountant sought the assistance of the former Acting Municipal Accountant to determine/identify the concerned creditor and other details of the payables amounting to P7,183.82. It turned out as a valid claim of a former employee for the reimbursement of her traveling expenses. Reversion of the Accounts Payable, aged two years and above, to the Unappropriated Surplus is not necessary because the related payables have been determined valid and enforceable.
observations.reason: Payables totaling P385,169.39 are staled checks which have been determined to be a valid transaction way back in CYs 2021 and 2022. They have been communicated to the concerned payee/s for the replacement of the staled checks.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor prioritize the prompt and timely implementation of projects funded out of the grants from NGAs, in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the necessary liquidation documents, as soon as the intended projects have been duly completed, for the eventual settlement with the concerned SAs; and
b. Municipal Treasurer to cause the refund of the unexpended balances of the completed PPAs, and the prompt remittance of the share of various Local Government Units from collections made by the LGU, directly to the concerned SAs or to the Bureau of Treasury, pursuant to COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.managementAction:
Implemented
The Municipal Agricultural Officer had communicated with the DA – Regional Office requesting for a change in the intended program for the unutilized funds. They are still waiting for the approval of the DA – Regional Office. The unutilized funds totaling P1,000,000.00 for the cashew production had been duly returned to the DA – Regional Office in the first quarter of 2025. The unexpended balance of the project, Construction of Local Access Road in Barangay Harrison amounting to P667,284.18 had been refunded to the concerned Source Agency (SA). The Management communicated with the concerned SAs seeking approval to re-program the remaining balances to other PPAs. Meanwhile, shares of various NGAs from collections made by the LGU had not yet been remitted because the Municipal Treasurer is still trying to identify the concerned NGAs where the collection is due.
observations.reason: Some of the unexpended balances had been refunded (e.g., P1,000,000.00 returned to DA; P667,284.18 refunded to Source Agency). Others still pending due to coordination and awaiting approvals from the concerned Source Agencies.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to verify/identify the nature and details of the outstanding Other Payables account totaling P2.931 million, and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries for the noted errors in recording, for fair and accurate presentation of the affected Payable accounts in the Statement of Financial Position as at year-end.
observations.managementAction:
Implemented
The Municipal Accountant already coordinated with the former Acting Municipal Accountant for the details of said payables. The Municipal Accountant could not determine the nature and details of the outstanding Other Payables, as these are outstanding balances carried forward in the previous financial reports of the former Acting Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: The concerned Municipal officials have yet to submit the other lacking documents. The outstanding balances are carried forward from previous administration records; coordination with former Acting Municipal Accountant is ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) together with the concerned Municipal Officials, provide a reasonable explanation on the noted inconsistencies in the procurement activities of the LGU; (b) henceforth ensure that all procurement activities undertaken by the LGU are strictly made in accordance with the provisions of the updated 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, to ensure the regularity of the procurement process and the award of the contracts; and (c) consider the procurement of common-use goods, supplies, materials and equipment from the DBM-PS.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. BAC, BAC Secretariat and the TWG to: (i) immediately submit all the lacking Bidding Documents to the Municipal Accountant, for the eventual submission to the Audit Team; (ii) be cautious during the bid evaluation and post-qualification phase, for proper evaluation and assessment of the eligibility, as to the legal, technical and financial capacities of the prospective bidders; and (iii) revisit the updated GPPB guidelines, particularly the prescribed format of the bidding forms, and adopt the same in the succeeding procurements;
b. BAC to ensure that all perfected contracts and its supporting documents are submitted to the Audit Team, five days after perfection, pursuant to Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001, to facilitate auditorial review thereof;
c. Municipal Accountant to: (i) immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents, including the lacking perfected contracts and their respective supporting documents, for verification, and henceforth, ensure that all claims are duly supported with proper and complete supporting documents, as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001, before processing for payment; (ii) ensure prompt and complete submission of all financial documents, records and reports, within the prescribed period, to facilitate timely verification thereof; and (iii) prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to record the noted errors in recording, for fair and accurate presentation of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements; and
d. Municipal Engineer to: (i) ensure that detailed engineering activities on all infrastructure projects are sufficiently carried-out, including the computation of the contract duration, pursuant to Annex A of the updated 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; (ii) immediately submit the lacking technical documents to the Audit Team to facilitate the technical review, evaluation and inspection of the TAG; and (iii) ascertain that relevant information on all infrastructure projects are duly posted on the project billboards, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004; and
e. Municipal Treasurer to require the payees and/or their authorized representatives to indicate their printed names in the Box D portion of the DVs, to facilitate verification, and see to it that payments are received by the duly authorized representatives only.
observations.managementAction:
Implemented
The Municipal Mayor, BAC, BAC Secretariat and the TWG submitted written explanations on the noted deficiencies and inconsistencies. Advance copies of the perfected contracts were submitted directly to the Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant and BAC Secretariat submitted the lacking documents to COA and prepared the necessary adjusting journal entries. The Municipal Engineer submitted responses and the required engineering activities are being monitored. The Municipal Treasurer instructed the Disbursing Officer to ensure printed names are indicated in the Box D portion of the DVs and to monitor payments to payees/authorized representatives.
observations.reason: Some lacking bidding documents were submitted to COA; many deficiencies were addressed with explanations and some documentation; however, some supporting documents are still outstanding and being coordinated for submission.

observations.managementAction:

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure strict compliance with the pertinent laws, rules and regulations on the monitoring, utilization, implementation and reporting of COVID-19 related PPAs. Likewise, the Municipal Mayor was advised to require the:
Acting Municipal Accountant to: (i) immediately submit to the Audit Team the noted lacking supporting documents, for verification, and henceforth, ensure that all claims are duly supported with proper and complete supporting documents before processing for payment; and (ii) ensure that purchases of relief items, medical supplies and materials are recorded under the appropriate Inventory accounts upon purchase, and expensed only upon issuance thereof, and all equipment to its appropriate PPE accounts, for fair and accurate presentation in the Financial Statements;
Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that all procurements for COVID-19 related PPAs are made strictly in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly in the award of contracts, to ensure that these are awarded to eligible bidders/suppliers; and
Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the printed names of the claimants are duly indicated in the Box D portion of the disbursement vouchers before the release of payments thereof.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal Accountant to: (i) prepare and submit the BRS for LBP Account No. 0882-1099-41, under the Trust Fund – Philhealth books; (ii) ensure that the book and bank balances reflected in the BRS are reconciled with the ledger and bank statement balances; and (iii) ensure the prompt recording of receipts/deposits in the books, for fair and correct presentation of the Cash in Bank - LCCA balance in the Statement of Financial Position.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer: (i) ensure that all collections are deposited intact to the depository banks daily or on the next banking day; and                        (ii) immediately transfer the 20 percent share from Internal Revenue Allotment to the respective bank account for the 20 percent Development Fund to avoid any delays in the implementation of programs, projects and activities therein.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to: (a) conduct a complete physical count on all PPEs of the Municipality, prepare and submit the corresponding duly accomplished RPCPPEs to the Audit Team, copy furnished the Acting Municipal Accountant for proper reconciliation with the accounting records, and henceforth, ensure that said physical count are strictly and timely conducted every year; (b) see to it that PPEs reported in the RPCPPEs are classified into its proper PPE accounts and that RPCPPEs are prepared in accordance with Annex 49 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume II; and (c) prepare a Physical Inventory Plan, and ensure that the inventory taking is completed in accordance with the guidelines and procedures prescribed under COA Circular No 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts of the Municipality.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
Acting Property Officer and the Acting Municipal Accountant to: (i) evaluate and analyze the discrepancies in the PPE accounts amounting to P293,463,746.82; (ii) reconcile and make the necessary adjustments to the reported PPE account balances in the Statement of Financial Position and the submitted RPCPPEs; and (iii) maintain complete and updated perpetual inventory records, which should always be reconciled, any discrepancy, shall be properly evaluated to facilitate adjustments on the affected PPE accounts, for fair and accurate presentation in the Statement of Financial Position;
Acting Property Officer to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), together with the required supporting documents, for submission to the Audit Team, to facilitate the request for the disposal of the unserviceable items; and
Acting Municipal Accountant to: (i) prepare the necessary adjusting Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to record in the General Fund books, the service vehicle, watercrafts and communication equipment totaling P10,134,010.00; and           (ii) verify whether the projects undertaken By-Administration totaling P10,033,596.00 are already completed, make the necessary adjustments on the noted errors in recording, and henceforth ensure that projects undertaken By-Administration are properly taken up in the books, for fair and correct presentation of the account balances in the Financial Statements.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) prioritize the implementation of projects funded out of grants from various NGAs; and                     (b) require the Acting Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary liquidation documents as soon as the projects have been duly completed and/or cause the refund of the unexpended balances of the completed PPAs to the Bureau of Treasury and/or the concerned SAs, pursuant to COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal Accountant to: (a) analyze the unreconciled balances of the Due from and Due to Other Funds account, identify the reconciling items and prepare the necessary adjusting Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to reflect the correct account balances in the Statement of Financial Position; and (b) strictly monitor the reciprocal accounts by conducting regular reconciliation thereof, to eliminate and avoid future discrepancies, if any.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the Municipal Planning and Development Council, ensure the optimal utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund to spur development, provide jobs to constituents and to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU, so that the intended users/beneficiaries may enjoy the immediate benefits derived from said development projects.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
Municipal Budget Officer and Acting Municipal Accountant to: (i) evaluate the idle appropriations and the unexpended balances from previously completed projects, if no longer needed, consider consolidating said funds to augment/fund other priority PPAs of the LGU; (ii) analyze and reconcile the noted errors in the SAAOB, and make the necessary adjustments therein, for accurate and reliable reporting; and (iii) ensure that sources of funds available for appropriation are clearly identified, particularly the PPAs being realigned;
Acting Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit Team, the noted lacking supporting documents for verification, and henceforth, ensure that all claims are duly supported with proper and complete supporting documents before processing for payment to establish the validity and propriety of the disbursements; and
Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation of the target programs, projects and activities under the 20% Development Fund, particularly those projects already with available construction materials, to prevent further deterioration and/or loss thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
MDRRM Council to: (i) ensure the optimal utilization of the LDRRMF, to strengthen and enhance the capacity and resilience of the LGU and the constituents in addressing and/or responding to calamities, disasters and emergency situations; (ii) adhere to the provision of DBM Budget Circular No. 2017-01 dated April 26, 2017 on the acquisition of service vehicle to be used for disaster response and rescue/relief operations; and (iii) ascertain that PPAs charged against the LDRRMF are related to the key objectives of disaster risk reduction and management activities, pursuant to the provisions of the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1;
MDRRMO to incorporate in the LDRRMFIP, the list of priority programs, projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended balances under the Special Trust Fund, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
Acting Municipal Accountant to: (i) make the necessary revisions to the CY 2021 Report on Sources and Utilizations of the DRRMF, to include the funds received from the NDRRMC; and (ii) immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents for verification, and henceforth, ensure that claims are duly supported with complete and proper documentation before processing for payment to establish the validity, regularity and propriety of the disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the GAD Focal Point System: (a) cause the optimal and judicious utilization of the GAD Funds, pursuant to the provisions of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by JMC No. 2016-01, to achieve the objective of providing equal development opportunities for men and women, through gender responsive PPAs; and (b) see to it that GAD Accomplishment Report is duly submitted to the Audit Team within five working days from end of January of the preceding year, pursuant to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001, to facilitate assessment and evaluation thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure strict compliance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations on the monitoring, utilization and reporting of the status of funds and the implementation of COVID-19 related PPAs and see to it that charges out of the 20% DF are only those explicitly allowed under DILG-DBM JMC No. 01. Likewise, the Municipal Mayor was advised to require the:

BAC to ensure that all procurements for COVID-19 related PPAs are included in the supplemental APPs and revised AIPs approved by the local sanggunian, and see to it that all procurements are strictly in accordance with the modes of procurement indicated therein; and

Acting Municipal Accountant to: (i) immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents, to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that all claims are duly supported with proper and complete supporting documents before processing for payment;         (ii) ensure that purchases of relief goods for distribution are recorded under its proper inventory accounts, appropriate expense accounts shall be recognized only upon its issuance; and (iii) prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the adjustments on the noted errors in recording, for fair and accurate presentation of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction:

Likewise, the Management also submitted proofs that the required Bayanihan Grant Reports for the period April 2020 to December 2020, had been emailed to the DBM.

Also, lists for the distribution of the palay, rice, assorted vegetables and relief goods were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (i) ensure that all procurement activities undertaken by the Municipality is strictly in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, to ensure the regularity of the procurement process and the award of the contracts; (ii) consider procurement of common-use goods, supplies, materials and equipment from the DBM-PS; and (iii) ascertain that the LGU is fully equipped and has enough capacity to implement the infrastructure projects “By Administration”, pursuant to Appendix 1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

BAC and the TWG to:             (i) immediately submit to the Acting Municipal Accountant all the lacking documents in the Bidding Documents and other related documents pertaining to the award of the contracts, and issue a reasonable explanation on the noted deficiencies and inconsistencies in the award of the contracts; (ii) adopt the latest edition of the Philippine Bidding Documents, as prescribed by the GPPB; and (iii) be vigilant and cautious, particularly during the bid evaluation and post-qualification phase, for proper evaluation and assessment of the eligibility as to legal, technical and financial capacities of the prospective bidders;

Acting Municipal Accountant to: (i) immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents, to arrive at a decision in audit, and ensure that all claims are duly supported with proper and complete supporting documents before processing for payment, as required under Sections 9.1, 9.1.1, and 9.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001; (ii) ensure that purchases of relief items, supplies and materials are recorded under the appropriate Inventory accounts and recognize the appropriate expense accounts only upon its issuance; and    (iii) prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to account for the noted errors in recording for fair and accurate presentation of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements; and

Municipal Engineer to:           (i) ensure that detailed engineering activities on all infrastructure projects are sufficiently carried-out pursuant to Annex A of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, and submit the same to the Audit Team to facilitate the TAS in their technical review, evaluation and inspection;     (ii) closely monitor the project implementations, ensure that projects are completed within the contract duration;           (iii) ensure the accuracy of the information contained in the Statement of Work Accomplished and Time Elapsed and other related technical documents; and              (iv) ascertain that relevant information on all infrastructure projects are duly posted on the project billboards, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004.

observations.recommendation:

observations.managementAction: The outstanding Due to BIR as of December 31, 2020 amounting to P1,417,113.59 were already paid by the LGU in February 2021 and March 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, being the Chairperson of the Municipal DRRM Council, (i) cause for the optimal utilization of the LDRRM Fund to strengthen the resilience of the constituents to disaster and climate change impacts.  Disbursement against the fund should be in pursuance of RA No. 10121; and (ii) prioritize the establishment of the Local DRRM Office which will be utilized as the office of the DRRM personnel and shall also serve as a single storage area for DRRM equipages for better upkeep and maintenance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor together with the Municipal Ecological Solid Waste Management Board prioritize the development of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan and ensure that all members of the Board are duly represented pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 9003; oversee and ensure its effective implementation and review the plan every two (2) years, thereafter, to ensure the protection of public health and the environment.
observations.reason:
The MESWMB still failed to make the necessary revisions to the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan for review of the DENR-EMB, thus, were still not approved by the NSWMC.

In a letter dated June 07, 2021, the Municipal Mayor informed that close coordination with the DENR-EMB revealed that the submitted documents has been misplaced, therefore no comments/findings were received. Nonetheless, they committed to update the WACS, and submit the revised MSWMP to the DENR-EMB.

The foregoing is discussed in the Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal GFPS and its TWG to: 

revisit the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by 2016-01, in the preparation of GPB to properly plan, avoid delays and evaluate whether gender mainstreaming was undertaken in all PPAs and attributed to the GPB; 

prioritize the establishment of GAD database as it will be the basis in determining gender issues, articulating gender gaps between men and women and conducting gender analysis; and

cause the optimal and judicious utilization of the GAD Fund pursuant to Section 5.b.iv of the PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01, to achieve the objective of providing equal development opportunities to men and women through gender responsive PPAs.
observations.reason:
The GFPS still failed to prepare and submit the GAD Plan and Budget within the prescribed timelines, thus, resulted in the late endorsement of the GPB.

Also, the Municipality still failed to optimally utilize the GAD budget, registering a financial delivery rate of 30.73 percent only in CY 2020, due to the COVID-19 Pandemic and the implementation of the community quarantines in the entire of Province of Occidental Mindoro, prohibiting social interactions, thus, several programs had been cancelled.

The foregoing is discussed in the Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor administer effectively the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund to spur development, provide jobs to the local constituents and to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.
observations.reason:
The Municipality still failed to efficiently utilize the 20% DF, registering a financial delivery rate of 42.88 percent only in CY 2020. 

The Management has yet to provide their compliance and/or comments and explanations on the matter.

The other noted deficiencies in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund are discussed in detail in the Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure strict compliance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations on the monitoring, utilization and reporting of the status of funds and the implementation of COVID-19 related PPAs and see to it that charges out of the 20% DF are only those explicitly allowed under DILG-DBM JMC No. 01. Likewise, the Municipal Mayor was advised to require the:
BAC to ensure that all procurements for COVID-19 related PPAs are included in the supplemental APPs and revised AIPs approved by the local sanggunian, and see to it that all procurements are strictly in accordance with the modes of procurement indicated therein; and
Acting Municipal Accountant to: (i) immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents, to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that all claims are duly supported with proper and complete supporting documents before processing for payment; (ii) ensure that purchases of relief goods for distribution are recorded under its proper inventory accounts, appropriate expense accounts shall be recognized only upon its issuance; and (iii) prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the adjustments on the noted errors in recording, for fair and accurate presentation of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal Accountant to: (a) prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the adjustments on the noted errors in recording, for fair and accurate presentation of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements; and (b) conduct regular reconciliation of the outstanding loan balances and interest payments with the lending institutions, to avoid overpayments and/or errors in recording.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) prioritize the implementation of projects funded out of the grants from various NGAs; and              (b) require the Acting Municipal Accountant to: (i) evaluate the need for those unexpended balances from various NGAs, if still needed, consider making representations with the respective SAs for the authority to use the remaining funds for other related projects of the Municipality, otherwise, cause the remittance of the full amount to the Bureau of Treasury and/or the concerned SAs, pursuant to Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013; and (ii) prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust the noted error in recording, for fair and accurate presentation of the Due to NGAs account balance in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal Accountant to: (a) analyze the unreconciled balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds account, identify the reconciling items and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct account balances in the Statement of Financial Position; and (b) strictly monitor the reciprocal accounts by conducting regular reconciliation to eliminate and avoid any discrepancies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (i) ensure that all procurement activities undertaken by the Municipality is strictly in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, to ensure the regularity of the procurement process and the award of the contracts; (ii) consider procurement of common-use goods, supplies, materials and equipment from the DBM-PS; and (iii) ascertain that the LGU is fully equipped and has enough capacity to implement the infrastructure projects “By Administration”, pursuant to Appendix 1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.  Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
BAC and the TWG to: (i) immediately submit to the Acting Municipal Accountant all the lacking documents in the Bidding Documents and other related documents pertaining to the award of the contracts, and issue a reasonable explanation on the noted deficiencies and inconsistencies in the award of the contracts; (ii) adopt the latest edition of the Philippine Bidding Documents, as prescribed by the GPPB; and (iii) be vigilant and cautious, particularly during the bid evaluation and post-qualification phase, for proper evaluation and assessment of the eligibility as to legal, technical and financial capacities of the prospective bidders;
Acting Municipal Accountant to: (i) immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents, to arrive at a decision in audit, and ensure that all claims are duly supported with proper and complete supporting documents before processing for payment, as required under Sections 9.1, 9.1.1, and 9.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001; (ii) ensure that purchases of relief items, supplies and materials are recorded under the appropriate Inventory accounts and recognize the appropriate expense accounts only upon its issuance; and (iii) prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to account for the noted errors in recording for fair and accurate presentation of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements; and
Municipal Engineer to: (i) ensure that detailed engineering activities on all infrastructure projects are sufficiently carried-out pursuant to Annex A of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, and submit the same to the Audit Team to facilitate the TAS in their technical review, evaluation and inspection; (ii) closely monitor the project implementations, ensure that projects are completed within the contract duration; (iii) ensure the accuracy of the information contained in the Statement of Work Accomplished and Time Elapsed and other related technical documents; and              (iv) ascertain that relevant information on all infrastructure projects are duly posted on the project billboards, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (i) ensure that all procurement activities undertaken by the Municipality is strictly in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, to ensure the regularity of the procurement process and the award of the contracts; (ii) consider procurement of common-use goods, supplies, materials and equipment from the DBM-PS; and (iii) ascertain that the LGU is fully equipped and has enough capacity to implement the infrastructure projects “By Administration”, pursuant to Appendix 1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
BAC and the TWG to: (i) immediately submit to the Acting Municipal Accountant all the lacking documents in the Bidding Documents and other related documents pertaining to the award of the contracts, and issue a reasonable explanation on the noted deficiencies and inconsistencies in the award of the contracts; (ii) adopt the latest edition of the Philippine Bidding Documents, as prescribed by the GPPB; and (iii) be vigilant and cautious, particularly during the bid evaluation and post-qualification phase, for proper evaluation and assessment of the eligibility as to legal, technical and financial capacities of the prospective bidders;
Acting Municipal Accountant to: (i) immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents, to arrive at a decision in audit, and ensure that all claims are duly supported with proper and complete supporting documents before processing for payment, as required under Sections 9.1, 9.1.1, and 9.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001; (ii) ensure that purchases of relief items, supplies and materials are recorded under the appropriate Inventory accounts and recognize the appropriate expense accounts only upon its issuance; and (iii) prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to account for the noted errors in recording for fair and accurate presentation of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements; and
Municipal Engineer to: (i) ensure that detailed engineering activities on all infrastructure projects are sufficiently carried-out pursuant to Annex A of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, and submit the same to the Audit Team to facilitate the TAS in their technical review, evaluation and inspection; (ii) closely monitor the project implementations, ensure that projects are completed within the contract duration; (iii) ensure the accuracy of the information contained in the Statement of Work Accomplished and Time Elapsed and other related technical documents; and              (iv) ascertain that relevant information on all infrastructure projects are duly posted on the project billboards, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the BAC: (a) conduct meticulous and judicious procurement planning to forestall emergency purchases and minimize, if not to totally avoid, payments on reimbursement basis; (b) prepare the APP-CSE for submission to the DBM-PS; and (c) ensure that procurements of the Municipality, as far as practicable, undergo the competitive public bidding, alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted to only in highly exceptional cases provided in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
We also recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant ensure that all claims are duly supported with proper documentation, complete with all the necessary details and information such as, the purpose and the specific items reimbursed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairperson of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board: (a) reconstitute the composition of the Board to conform with the membership requirement of RA No. 9003, to optimize the involvement of civil society as envisioned by the law; (b) prioritize the development of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan for review of the DENR-EMB and subsequent approval of the NSWMC, and henceforth, oversee and ensure its effective implementation to protect public health and the environment, pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 9003; and (c) ensure optimal utilization of funds appropriated for programs, projects and activities related to Solid Waste Management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the Municipal Planning and Development Council, ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund to spur development, provide jobs to constituents and to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU, so that the intended users/beneficiaries may enjoy the immediate benefits from said development projects. Likewise, we also recommended that the:
Municipal Budget Officer evaluate the idle appropriations for loan amortizations and the unexpended balances from completed projects, if no longer necessary, shall be pooled together to augment the funding requirements for other priority programs, projects and activities of the Municipality;
Acting Municipal Accountant make the necessary revision to the CY 2020 SAOB for the 20% Development Fund by including the P9,955,465.72 re-aligned funds for medical supplies, equipment, relief goods and other related COVID-19 expenses; and
Municipal Engineer fast track the implementation of the target programs, projects and activities under the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the GAD Focal Point System: (a) ensure that the GPB are prepared and submitted to the PPDO for review and subsequently to the DILG Provincial Office for its endorsement, within the prescribed timelines, to avoid delays in the project implementation; and (b) cause the optimal and judicious utilization of the GAD Funds, pursuant to the provisions of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by JMC No. 2016-01, to achieve the objective of providing equal development opportunities for men and women, through a gender responsive PPAs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the other Department Heads, practice austerity measures in the government, especially during this time of pandemic and evaluate the necessity and reasonableness of the travels before approval thereof, to ensure that government funds are used only for worthwhile programs, projects and activities that will benefit the constituents.
Likewise the Municipal Mayor is advised to require the Acting Municipal Accountant to: (i) immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that claims are duly supported with complete and proper documentation before processing claims for payment to establish the validity and propriety of the disbursements; (ii) ensure that grants of travel allowances, particularly those that have not yet been incurred, are treated and accounted for as cash advance; and (iii) prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the adjustments on the noted errors in recording, for fair and accurate presentation of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Acting Municipal Accountant to ensure regular and prompt remittance of the taxes withheld to the BIR within 10 days after the end of each month, pursuant to the BIR Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007, to avoid penalties, interests and surcharges imposed on late remittances.

We recommended for the immediate settlement of the audit suspension within 90 days from receipt of the Notice of Suspension, pursuant to Section 9.4 of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts (RRSA). The head of the agency is responsible for ensuring that the requirements of transactions suspended in audit are complied with. Also, we recommended that the persons held liable for ND Nos. 2014-013-101-(13) to 2014-033-101-(13) totaling P31,334,183.74 take the appropriate actions in compliance with the said COA Decision.

reclassify the completed infrastructure projects from CIP accounts to the appropriate PPE accounts; and
