observations.recommendation:
We recommended that (a) the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory
Committee who shall conduct the physical count of all PPE, submit a complete
and accurate report thereon, copy furnish the Acting Municipal Accountant to
facilitate reconciliation with accounting records; (b) the designated Property
Officer maintain complete and updated perpetual inventory records which
should always reconcile with the PPE general ledger balances; and (c) the Acting
Municipal Accountant examine any discrepancy between the Accounting and
property records to facilitate adjustment of the affected PPE account balances
for their fair presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant (i) maintain the
complete subsidiary ledger for the Loans Payable - Domestic account for easy
verification, monitoring and reconciliation; and (b) exert effort to reconcile the
balance of Loans Payable to come up with the correct amount of loans for its fair
presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant ensure that
payments of labor payrolls are supported with complete and proper
documentary requirements pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No.
2012-001. We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan members
perform their legislative functions within their authority limit in accordance
with Section 447 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the (a) Personnel Officer and all concerned
personnel strictly adhere to the Rules and Regulations of the Civil Service
Commission in processing application of monetization of leave credits and
terminal leave of employees; (b) Acting Municipal Accountant ensure the
completeness of supporting documents as provided in COA Circular No. 2012-
001 dated June 14, 2012 before processing claims for payment to establish the
regularity, validity and propriety of the disbursements.

We recommended that the Management, particularly the (a) Bids and
Awards Committee strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its
Revised IRR with regard to procurement of infrastructure projects to establish
the regularity, validity and propriety of the disbursements; (b) the Acting
Municipal Accountant submit the complete documentation prescribed under
Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to facilitate review of contract; (b)
Municipal Engineer prepare and submit the required documents during and
after the contract implementation, such as, inspection report, statement of time
elapsed, plan/drawings and see to it that the Statement of Work Accomplished
submitted by the contractor are properly dated and the billboards containing
information of the project are installed by the contractor; and (c) the
Inspectorate Team inspect the reported work accomplished of the contractor to
be able to ascertain if delays were incurred.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Engineer compute the
Approved Budget for the Contract pursuant to the DPWH Department Order
No. 22, as amended by DPWH DO No. 197, series of 2016, closely monitor/
supervise project implementation to ensure contractor’s compliance with the
specifications of the contract and submit the required documents for the
Construction of Dikes at Brgy. I - Mapalad; and (b) Municipal Project
Inspectorate Team to enforce immediate corrective measures relative to the cost
deficiencies noted in the conduct of technical inspection to avoid disallowance in
audit.

We recommended that the Management, particularly the (a) Bids and
Awards Committee (i) strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its
2016 Revised IRR on the procurement of goods; (ii) see that legal, technical and
financial documents are completely submitted by the bidders; and (iii) properly
conduct bid evaluation and post-qualification to ensure regularity and propriety
of award of contract; (b) the Acting Municipal Accountant (i) submit the lacking
supporting documents and (ii) check the regularity and completeness of
supporting documents prescribed under Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-
001 to facilitate review of contract, determine compliance with RA No. 9184 and
to establish the legality, validity and propriety of the disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) direct all offices
concerned to prepare PPMP for consolidation by the BAC Secretariat into an
APP containing all the requirements of the Municipality, the procurement
schedule and the mode of procurement; (b) require the BAC to prepare and
submit APP in accordance with the format approved by the GPPB and to
prepare the APP-CSE for submission to DBM-PS; (c) consider procuring
common-used goods, supplies, materials and equipment from the DBM-PS; (d)
require the BAC to subject procurements, as far as practicable, to public
bidding unless the conditions are present for the use of shopping or small value
procurement as alternative mode of procurement; (e) comply strictly with
Section 54.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 on the prohibition of splitting of
contract; and (f) procurement planning should be done meticulously to avoid
payments on reimbursement basis.
We also recommended that the Municipality through the (a) BAC (i) see
to it that procurement are made through public bidding to obtain the most
advantageous cost to the Municipality; (ii) consolidate requisitions for goods
needed at or about the same time; (iii) observe strictly the prohibition on
reference to brand names; and (iv) alternative methods of procurement shall be
resorted to only in the highly exceptional cases provided in the Revised IRR of
RA No. 9184; (b) Acting Municipal Accountant (i) check the regularity and
completeness of supporting documents required to determine compliance with
RA No. 9184 and to establish the legality, validity and propriety of the
disbursements; and (ii) prepare journal entry voucher to record PPE totaling
P715,085.00 in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Management, particularly the Bids and
Awards Committee, strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its
Revised IRR on the procurement of consulting services and to submit the
complete documentation prescribed under Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-
001 to facilitate review of contract, determine compliance with RA No. 9184 and
to establish the legality, validity and propriety of the disbursements.

We recommended:
 For the optimal utilization of the LDRRM Fund to strengthen the
resilience of the constituents to disaster and climate change impacts.
Disbursement against the fund should be in pursuance of RA No.
10121;
 For the creation and filling up of the required four plantilla positions
to manage and operate the local MDDRM Office in compliance with
the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2014-01 dated April 4, 2014; and
 That the LDRRM Officer (a) attend and conduct trainings and
seminars to enhance the skill set of the MDRRMO personnel pursuant
to the Disaster Preparedness Minimum Standards; and (b) prepare
and submit inventory of existing MDRRM equipages to be able to
identify the lacking DRRM equipment, tools and paraphernalia and
utilize the LDRRM Fund for the acquisition of equipment in
compliance with the minimum standards and pursuant to DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2012-73.

We recommended that the Municipal Ecological Solid Waste
Management Board prioritize the development of the Municipal Solid Waste
Management Plan pursuant to the provisions of RA No. 9003, oversee and
ensure its effective implementation and review the plan every two years,
thereafter, to ensure the protection of public health and the environment.
We also recommended that the Management consider the eventual
closure of the open dumpsite and the establishment of sanitary landfill, in
compliance with the provisions of RA No. 9003 and allocate funds therefor. We
further recommend that the Management promote among its constituents waste
segregation at source, recycling and re-use of resources and consider
recommending to the Sangguniang Bayan to legislate no plastic policy in order
to reduce the volume of waste for collection and disposal.

We recommended to Management the utilization of the LCPC funds for
the implementation of priority programs, projects and activities embodied in the
prepared Annual Work and Financial Plan of the Municipality, in compliance
with Section 15 of RA No. 9344, otherwise known as the Juvenile Justice and
Welfare Act of 2006, so that the children of the Municipality could receive
maximum assistance, including proper care and nutrition and special protection
from all forms of conditions prejudicial to their development.

We recommended that the Management ensure that programs/projects/
activities to be charged against the 20% Development Fund are specifically
identified to facilitate proper implementation. Identify, first and foremost, the
programs and projects that must be prioritized for inclusion and consideration
in the Annual Investment Program. For those which are inconsistent with the
governing rules and regulations, initiate amendments to address the needs of the
constituents.
We also recommended that the Management faithfully carry out the
developmental objectives by which the 20% Development Fund has been created
and ensure optimal utilization of the fund for the implementation of projects for
the benefit of its constituents. The Acting Municipal Accountant see to it that all
the required supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers
and payrolls before processing payments.

We recommended that:
a. The Management, through the Bids and Awards Committee and the
Municipal Engineer, implement priority projects funded out of grants
from various entities in accordance with the specific purposes as
stated in the Memorandum of Agreements. The Municipal
Agriculturist to prioritize the implementation of programs/projects/
activities funded out of the financial assistance from BFAR;
b. The Municipal Accountant evaluate the need for those unexpended
fund balances from NGAs. Should the funds be proven no longer
needed, it should be remitted to the source agencies as required under
existing rules and regulations. If still needed, Management should
request authority from the source agency to use the money for other
related projects of the Municipality; and
c. The Municipal Accountant analyze the difference of P920,084.31 for
the fair representation of the account balance in the financial
statements and maintain complete subsidiary ledgers whenever
necessary pursuant to Section 111 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Management take the following courses of
action:
 The Acting Municipal Accountant maintain special accounts in the
General Fund for the operation of PMES to facilitate monitoring of its
operations and financial condition. These reports should form part of
the schedules of the General Fund;
 Install electric meters in the different local and national offices to
monitor and account for their electric consumptions and rule out
doubts on possible system loss; and
 The designated PMES Administrator require the preparation of
reports showing the kilowatts generated and distributed including
their costs to provide information for better appreciation and to
provide real time inputs for decision making purposes in improving
PMES operation and administration.

We recommended that the Municipality provide funds of at least 5% of
its annual budget specifically for the purpose to enable integration of GAD-
related activities in its regular operations and to submit the same to the PPDO
and the DILG for review and approval to ensure that the GAD programs,
projects, activities are gender responsive and aligned with the PPGD 1995-2025.
We also recommended that the Municipal GAD Focal Point oversee the proper
identification of GAD objectives and the immediate mainstreaming of GAD
programs, projects and activities that aim to address gender issues and concerns
in the Municipality.

receipt of the Notice of Suspension pursuant to Section 9.4 of the Rules and

ensuring that the requirements of transactions suspended in audit are complied with.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant ensure that all payments to job orders, casuals and contractual employees are properly supported with documents pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We also recommended for the issuance of contracts of service and/or job order contracts indicating the terms such as the daily wage rate, duration of work and work assignments/job description of each job order, casual and contractual employee to facilitate comparison with the actual accomplishment reports.

Also, the number of job order, casual, contractual employees should be kept reasonable, hence the need for the Human Resource Office to assess the actual workload and manpower requirement of the Municipality.  Consider filling up all vacant positions to augment existing workforce.
observations.managementAction: Hired at least 12 new regular employees.  Assigned personnel on rotational basis to lessen incurrence of overtime.
observations.reason: Oversight of the concerned municipal officials and employees in checking the completeness and propriety of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management comply strictly with the provisions of the MOA and pertinent rules and regulations to ensure efficiency and effectiveness of program implementation. We also recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant ensure that claims against government funds are supported with proper and complete documentary requirements to prove the validity, propriety and reasonableness of claims.
observations.managementAction: Accomplishments are being monitored by the DENR and reports thereof were already submitted to them.
observations.reason: Management believed all along that submission of supporting documents to DENR was enough, it being the source agency.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management take the following courses of action:


The Acting Municipal Accountant maintain special accounts in the General Fund for the operation of PMES to facilitate monitoring of its operations and financial condition. These reports should forms part of the schedules of the General Fund;

Install electric meters in the different local and national offices to monitor and account for their electric consumptions and rule out doubts on possible system loss; and 

The designated PMES Administrator require the preparation of reports showing the kilowatts generated and distributed including their costs to provide information for better appreciation and to provides real time inputs for decision making purposes in improving PMES operation and administration.
observations.managementAction: Net loss decreased by P3,362,942.58 during the year.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel in the Accounting Department to handle additional workload in maintaining special accounts in the General Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Management ensure that programs/projects/activities to be charged against the 20% Development Fund are specifically identified to facilitate proper implementation.  Identify, first and foremost, the programs and projects that must be prioritized for inclusion and consideration in the Annual Investment Plan.  For those which are inconsistent with the governing rules and regulations, initiate amendments to address the needs of the constituents;

The Management faithfully carry out the developmental objectives by which the 20% Development Fund has been created and ensure optimal utilization of the fund for the implementation of projects for the benefit of its constituents; and

Fund transfers be recorded in the books of accounts as receivable from the concerned barangays for proper liquidation to ensure that funds are used only for the intended purpose.
observations.managementAction: Transfers to barangays were limited to distribution of materials and these were paid through checks directly to suppliers.
observations.reason: Lack of available funds, thus, other expenditure items were charged against 20% Development Fund.
